item.title: US appeals court blocks Trump admin from enacting new plans to slash consumer watchdog staff
item.description: A federal appeals court on Friday blocked the Trump administration's plans to immediately slash the workforce at the U.S. Consumer Financial Protection Bureau by about two-thirds, delivering a setback to the White House's protracted efforts to shrink the consumer watchdog.

item.title: For US Vice President JD Vance, Iran talks could shape political rise
Chicago police investigate shooting that left 12 injured
US court rules Ohio can restrict children's use of social media
US Supreme Court limits ban on gun ownership by marijuana users
Mangione, accused CEO killer, withdraws mental health defense plans for now
Republicans blast Trump's Iran agreement as details emerge
How a warm World Cup welcome is endearing the US to fans
Maine Democrats pick progressive Dunlap in key House race after Golden exit
American Douglass breaks 50m freestyle world record
US acting spy chief seeks to fire hundreds of staff, CNN reports
US judge allows challenges to Trump's mail-in voting order ahead of November elections
Trump administration may alter slavery exhibit at Philadelphia site, court says
item.description: The ruling overturned a judge's injunction won by the city after the National Park Service removed an exhibit describing the ownership of enslaved people by George Washington.

item.title: Pentagon tells US lawmakers it needs $80 billion for Iran war and other bills, WSJ reports
item.description: The U.S. Department of Defense needs $80 billion to cover costs from the Iran war as well as other non-war-related bills, Deputy Defense Secretary Stephen Feinberg told lawmakers in phone calls this week, the Wall Street Journal reported on Thursday, citing people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Trump tells Axios he no longer views Anthropic as national security threat
item.description: Trump said he did not rule out using emergency powers against Anthropic.

item.title: Obama celebrates opening of his presidential museum with call to citizenship
item.description: Former U.S. President Barack Obama, celebrating a new museum and civic center dedicated to his eight years in the White House, called on Americans to resist the forces of political cynicism and division and instead embrace "our shared responsibilities as citizens."

item.title: Dali chief engineer reaches deferred prosecution deal over 2024 Key bridge collapse
item.description: The chief engineer of the cargo ship Dali that destroyed Baltimore’s Francis Scott Key Bridge in a March 2024 collision that killed six construction workers reached a deferred prosecution agreement, the government said on Thursday.

item.title: Texas's Camp Mystic had no evacuation plans on night of deadly floods, report finds
item.description: Camp Mystic, the Christian summer camp for girls in Texas where 28 people were killed in a 2025 flash flood, did not have written emergency evacuation plans and poorly trained its staff, according to a report released by the Texas Legislature on Thursday.

item.title: White House delays release of US voting machine study
item.description: The report does not say the vulnerabilities have led to votes flipping.

item.title: New Mexico Epstein probe subpoenas US Attorneys' offices
item.description: New Mexico legislators probing Jeffrey Epstein on Thursday subpoenaed U.S. Attorneys' offices in three states and the U.S. Virgin Islands for information on whether they chose not to prosecute the late sex offender after investigating his activities.
